spec_match.rationale: The page now exposes the headline API directly with a user-triggered `CSS.supports('at-rule(@name)')` probe plus the existing CSS `@supports at-rule(@name)` table.

interactive.rationale: Adds a custom at-rule input, a run button that invokes `CSS.supports('at-rule(...)')`, and a refresh button for the CSS/JS detection table.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (browser, detection, rule, supports, uses).

edge_cases.rationale: Empty or malformed custom input is surfaced as an invalid state instead of silently doing nothing. Browser-without-CSS.supports remains a simple null/unsupported branch.

references.rationale: The ChromeStatus link now sits in a labelled references section with corrected sibling links.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 27 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

summary: The detector now has a real runtime hook for `CSS.supports('at-rule(@name)')`, handles empty custom input, keeps the CSS-gated table, and includes a labelled references section.
